# Architecture Map

Purpose: describe how this repository's agentic system is organized and how it should operate inside host codebases.

## Goals
- Central source of truth for agent instructions.
- Installable into existing repositories with minimal friction.
- Auto-tailor agents/contexts to the host's tech stack.
- Surface inconsistencies early via `warnings.md`.
- Self-improve through failure analysis and instruction drift detection.

## System Components

### Kernel
- `.github/copilot-instructions.md` - Routes requests (task pipeline or free-form) to appropriate agents.

### Task Pipeline
- `raw.tasks.md` - Inbox for unrefined ideas
- `tasks.md` - Structured backlog with IDs, priorities, agents, modes
- `failed.tasks.md` - Post-mortems with failure analysis

### Health & Evolution
- `warnings.md` - Inconsistencies, risks, technical debt
- `.github/docs/instruction-changelog.md` - Version history of instruction changes

### Meta-Agents (System Management)
| Agent | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `.github/agents/onboarding.agent.md` | Scan repo, detect stack, generate tailored agents/contexts |
| `.github/agents/task-creator.agent.md` | Convert raw tasks to structured tasks |
| `.github/agents/task-priority-planner.agent.md` | Order, batch, and track task state |
| `.github/agents/task-runner.agent.md` | Execute tasks with domain agents |
| `.github/agents/instruction-editor.agent.md` | Update instructions with feedback loop |
| `.github/agents/instruction-drift.agent.md` | Detect instruction decay, propose fixes |

### General-Purpose Agents (Ad-hoc Help)
| Agent | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `.github/agents/assistant.agent.md` | Q&A, explanations, general help |
| `.github/agents/code-review.agent.md` | Code quality review, PR review |
| `.github/agents/design.agent.md` | Architecture decisions, ADRs, pattern selection |
| `.github/agents/debug.agent.md` | Error diagnosis, root cause analysis |
| `.github/agents/refactor.agent.md` | Safe code restructuring |
| `.github/agents/docs.agent.md` | Documentation creation and improvement |
| `.github/agents/testing.agent.md` | Test strategy, coverage gaps |
| `.github/agents/security.agent.md` | Vulnerability review, secure coding |
| `.github/agents/performance.agent.md` | Optimization, bottleneck analysis |
| `.github/agents/migration.agent.md` | Framework upgrades, breaking changes |

### Domain Agents (Stack-Specific)
| Agent | Stack |
|-------|-------|
| `.github/agents/auth.agent.md` | Firebase, Auth0, OIDC authentication |
| `.github/agents/frontend.agent.md` | React, Vue, Angular frontends |
| `.github/agents/feature.creator.agent.md` | Backend features, APIs, endpoints |
| `.github/agents/quality.csharp.agent.md` | C# code quality and patterns |
| `.github/agents/quality.ts.agent.md` | TypeScript code quality |
| `.github/agents/aspire.tests.integration.agent.md` | .NET Aspire integration testing |
| `.github/agents/deployment.compose.agent.md` | Docker Compose setup |
| `.github/agents/terraform.agent.md` | Infrastructure as Code |

### Contexts
| Context | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `.github/contexts/project.patterns.md` | Naming, structure, conventions (generated by onboarding) |
| `.github/contexts/auth.context.md` | Auth provider, flows, middleware |
| `.github/contexts/frontend.context.md` | Framework, state mgmt, styling |
| `.github/contexts/aspire.context.md` | Aspire setup, test harness |
| `.github/contexts/terraform.context.md` | Providers, modules, state mgmt |

## How to Extend
1. Add a new Context in `.github/contexts/` describing the domain.
2. Add a Domain Agent in `.github/agents/` using the Agent Template Schema (see `.github/agents/onboarding.agent.md`).
3. Update this file with the new agent/context.
4. Add to Stack Detection Matrix in `.github/agents/onboarding.agent.md` if auto-detection needed.
5. Add warnings for known gaps in `warnings.md`.

## Host Repository Notes
*This section is populated during onboarding with project-specific details.*

- Tech stack:
- Service boundaries:
- Naming conventions:
- Deployment model:
- Key docs/links:
